name: Anne Parelkar
education: BA in History (Lyon College, 2005); JD (William H. Bowen School of Law, University of Arkansas at Little Rock, 2011)
family: Married to Nikhil Parelkar, a first-generation immigrant; two children
whoTheyAre:
Anne Parelkar is an immigration attorney based in Overland Park who is running for the U.S. Senate for the first time. She was born in Pine Bluff, Arkansas, raised in White Hall, Arkansas, and earned a history degree from Lyon College in 2005 before graduating from law school at the University of Arkansas at Little Rock in 2011. She is licensed to practice law in Kansas, Arkansas, and Missouri, and is a member of the American Immigration Lawyers Association. She married her husband Nikhil, a first-generation immigrant, shortly after passing the bar, and they moved to Overland Park together in 2017.

Parelkar says she decided to run after traveling across Kansas to attend one of U.S. Sen. Roger Marshall's town halls in western Kansas. She has said that watching how Marshall handled constituent concerns about transparency and communication was what pushed her into the race. Her campaign leans heavily on her professional background in immigration law and on the argument that she has seen the failures of the federal immigration system up close through her daily legal work.

She has no prior elected office and no previous campaigns on her record. In a Democratic primary field of eight candidates, she is the only immigration attorney, which gives her a distinct professional niche even as she competes for the same Johnson County voter base as Sandy Spidel Neumann.
theirRecord:
Parelkar's public record is built on her legal career rather than any time in elected office. She has practiced immigration law for more than a decade, working with families navigating a system she describes as confusing and broken. Her most specific policy proposal is to replace the current patchwork of Medicaid and CHIP programs with a single consolidated health care program for everyone under 18. She also advocates for stronger antitrust and consumer protection laws and has made constituent transparency a central theme of her campaign.

On the other side of the ledger, Parelkar has no political track record to evaluate. She has never held office, never managed a government agency, and never cast a legislative vote. The National Republican Senatorial Committee filed a complaint in March 2026 naming her and three other Democratic Senate candidates for allegedly failing to file personal financial disclosure forms on time, as required by federal law. This is a procedural issue common among first-time candidates, not evidence of wrongdoing, but it gave opponents a talking point. Her campaign fundraising has also been modest -- through the end of 2025, her FEC filings showed just under $17,000 in total receipts, with $4,500 of that coming from candidate loans.
whatYouShouldKnow: Parelkar is the only immigration attorney in the eight-candidate Democratic Senate primary field, giving her unique professional credibility on one of the biggest issues in the 2026 cycle.
She has never held elected office or run for any political position before this Senate campaign.
She was born and raised in Arkansas and moved to Kansas through marriage in 2017. In a field where several candidates emphasize multi-generational Kansas roots, her transplant status stands out.
Her most detailed policy proposal is to create a single consolidated health care program for all Americans under 18, replacing the current patchwork of Medicaid, CHIP, and other coverage options.

The NRSC filed a complaint in March 2026 naming her and three other Democratic candidates for allegedly filing personal financial disclosures late. This is a procedural matter, not a corruption allegation.
She is based in Overland Park and competes for the same Johnson County Democratic voters as Sandy Spidel Neumann, splitting the suburban Kansas City lane in the primary.
Her total FEC-reported fundraising through the end of 2025 was $16,982, with $4,500 in candidate loans -- one of the smallest financial footprints among the declared Senate candidates.
whereTheyWorship: No church affiliation or congregation has been publicly identified for Anne Parelkar. Her campaign materials, public interviews, and media profiles focus on immigration law, family life, and children's health care rather than faith language or any named place of worship. Multiple research passes did not turn up a denominational connection or church membership in the public record.

quotes.text: We have a very big problem with immigration in our country right now, in the sense that there's a lot of confusion over it.
Answer to the people. Not to party leaders. Not to political donors. Just to the people.

campaignFinance.narrative: Parelkar's campaign has one of the smallest financial footprints in the Democratic Senate primary. Through December 31, 2025, her FEC filings show $16,982 in total receipts against $16,478.89 in disbursements, leaving just $503.11 cash on hand. Of that total, $4,500 came from candidate loans, meaning the majority of her outside fundraising came from small individual donations. No major donors or PAC contributions have been identified in the public record. Her FEC committee ID is C00913293.
campaignFinance.undisclosed: No individual donor breakdown is available in public FEC summary data. The bulk of non-loan receipts appear to be small individual contributions.
